title: fix(ui): flush a pending RichTextEditor edit on unmount instead of dropping it
body:
Fixes #840

## What

The unmount effect is commented *"Flush any pending debounce on unmount so the last edit is
never lost"*, but its body only cleared the timer — `emit` was never called from the cleanup,
so the pending markdown was discarded.

`RichTextEditor` is rendered conditionally (Preview / Edit / Source in `EditableOutput`), so
switching view unmounts it. Type, then click Preview within `ONCHANGE_DEBOUNCE_MS` (200 ms),
and the edit is gone with no error: the parent-owned `value` still holds the pre-keystroke
markdown, the preview/PDF renders stale text, and re-entering Edit re-parses that stale value.

## How

Capture the pending document synchronously in `onUpdate` and serialize **that** in the
cleanup:

```diff
+const pendingDocRef = useRef<PMNode | null>(null);

 onUpdate: ({ editor: ed }) => {
   setIsEmpty(ed.isEmpty);
+  pendingDocRef.current = ed.state.doc;
   if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
-  debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => emit(ed), ONCHANGE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
+  debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {
+    debounceRef.current = undefined;
+    const doc = pendingDocRef.current;
+    pendingDocRef.current = null;
+    if (doc) emitDoc(doc);
+  }, ONCHANGE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
 },
```

Two details worth flagging for review:

- **Why a doc snapshot rather than reading the editor in the cleanup.** A ProseMirror node is
  immutable, so keeping it is a pointer copy — and it means the flush never has to touch an
  editor Tiptap may already have destroyed by the time this component's cleanup runs.
- **No double-emit.** The timer path clears `pendingDocRef` *before* emitting, so an unmount
  immediately afterwards finds nothing left to send.

`emitDoc` is `useCallback([])`-stable, so `emit` stays stable and the existing
`useImperativeHandle(ref, …, [editor, emit])` dependency list is unaffected.

## Tests

New `RichTextEditor.unmountFlush.test.tsx`, driving **real** transactions through the real
editor via a toolbar command (typing into the ProseMirror surface needs layout APIs jsdom
does not implement; a toolbar command dispatches a genuine transaction, which is all
`onUpdate` needs):

- an edit made inside the debounce window is emitted when the component unmounts —
  **fails on `main`** (`expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 0 times`);
- the flush is value-**preserving** for an untouched document, so a plain view switch cannot
  mark it dirty with different content;
- a normally-fired debounce still emits exactly once, pinning the no-double-emit property.

Worth noting for the second test: the editor already schedules one emit on mount today —
loading the initial content is itself a transaction — so a mounted editor emits the unchanged
value after the debounce on `main` too. I verified that on unmodified `main` before writing
the assertion, so the test pins "never emits *different* content" rather than "never emits".

### CI determinism (updated)

The first revision of this suite was red on a slower CI runner even though it passed locally.
Two independent test-only causes, both now fixed (the production change is unchanged):

- **Mount-emit race.** Because loading the initial content schedules a debounce on mount, on a
  slow runner that mount timer could fire *before* the "nothing emitted yet" assertion under
  real timers. The suite now drains and clears that mount emit before making the edit under
  test, so the assertion only ever sees the edit's own timer.
- **Unhandled ProseMirror error.** jsdom implements no layout, so `Range.getClientRects` is
  `undefined` and ProseMirror's `coordsAtPos → singleRect` throws
  `target.getClientRects is not a function` from `scrollToSelection` on every transaction — one
  instance landing in the no-double-emit test. `Range.getClientRects`/`getBoundingClientRect`
  are now stubbed in the shared `vitest.setup.ts` (next to the existing `scrollIntoView` stub),
  so editor-driven tests no longer emit that unhandled error.

The three `unmountFlush` tests pass, with no unhandled errors, across repeated runs.

## Behavior note

Because the initial-content transaction populates `pendingDocRef`, a **fast mount → unmount**
inside the debounce window now fires one `onChange` even when the user typed nothing (previously
the cleanup only cleared the timer and emitted nothing). The emitted markdown round-trips to the
input value, so it is value-preserving — but any autosave or dirty-flag consumer downstream will
see that extra, same-value call and should treat a no-op change accordingly.

## Checklist

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` passes (`@ajh/ui`)
- [x] `eslint --max-warnings 0` clean on the touched files
- [x] `prettier` clean
- [x] Tests added
- [x] No IPC contract change, so no docs update needed

<sub>Note on local verification: the full `pnpm test` run is not green in my sandbox, but the
failures are pre-existing on unmodified `main` — 13 files / 146 tests fail with
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'setItem')` (no `localStorage` in this
environment). Verified by checking out clean `main` and re-running. CI is authoritative.</sub>

title: fix: RichTextEditor drops the last edit when the view is switched within the debounce window
body:
## Describe the bug

Anything typed in the rich-text editor during the last ~200 ms before switching away from
**Edit** is silently discarded.

### Root cause

`packages/ui/src/components/RichTextEditor/RichTextEditor.tsx`:

```tsx
// Flush any pending debounce on unmount so the last edit is never lost.
useEffect(() => {
  return () => {
    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
  };
}, []);
```

The comment promises a **flush**; the body only `clearTimeout`s. `emit` is never called from
the cleanup — its only two call sites are the debounce timeout itself and `replaceSelection`.
So when the component unmounts with a timer pending, the pending markdown is dropped and
`onChange` never fires.

```tsx
onUpdate: ({ editor: ed }) => {
  setIsEmpty(ed.isEmpty);
  if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
  debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => emit(ed), ONCHANGE_DEBOUNCE_MS);  // 200ms
},
```

### Why the editor unmounts

`EditableOutput` renders the three views conditionally:

```tsx
{view === 'edit' ? ( … <RichTextEditor … /> … ) : view === 'source' ? ( … <TextArea … /> … )
```

so a Preview / Edit / Source `SegmentedControl` switch unmounts `RichTextEditor` outright.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open a generated résumé or cover letter → **Edit**.
2. Type a few words — e.g. finish a sentence with `…reducing p99 latency by 60%`.
3. Within ~200 ms of the final keystroke, click **Preview** (or **Source**, or close the panel).
4. Click **Edit** again.

## Expected behaviour

The typed text is still there.

## Actual behaviour

The last edit is gone. The parent-owned canonical `value` still holds the pre-keystroke
markdown, so the preview/PDF renders stale text and re-entering Edit re-parses that stale
value. There is no error and no indication anything was lost.

The same loss happens on any unmount of the surrounding panel/modal inside the debounce
window — i.e. the very common "type, then immediately click away" pattern.

## Why nothing else catches it

No blur handler emits — `EditableOutput`'s `onBlur` only calls the caller's `onBlur?.()`, it
never reads the editor. `editor.destroy()` does not dispatch a transaction, and even if it
did, `onUpdate` only *re-schedules* the same debounce.

## Environment

- **OS**: platform-independent
- **App version**: `main` @ `e1a2295`

title: fix(scraper): delegate board location filtering to the central location_filter
body:
## Problem

Picking a city from the geocode suggestions returned **0 results** on the boards
that filter location client-side (The Muse, Comeet, GermanTechJobs). The location
box holds the picker's `"<city>, <country>"` label (`commands::geocoding` builds
it "so the UI only ever shows 'City, Country'"), but each board writes its own
shape — `"New York, NY"`, `"Munich, Bayern"`, `"Karl-Marx-Allee 1, Berlin"`. The
board-local filter required that **whole label** to appear as one contiguous
substring, which no board writes, so it dropped every row. Free-typed (unpicked)
input is one segment, which is why it survived manual testing.

## Fix (reworked per review)

The first revision added a board-local *segment-wise* matcher. Per review, this
version instead **removes board-local location filtering entirely** from the
three `supports_location() == false` boards and makes the engine's central
[`location_filter`](apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/scraping/engine/location_filter.rs)
the single authority on location — no second, divergent matcher.

Why this is correct and strictly better:

- These three boards are all non-location boards, so the engine **already** runs
  `location_filter` on their output, both as a live gate (`KeepItemByBoardFn`)
  and as a post-hoc safety net. The board-local location filter was redundant
  with it.
- The central filter is **diaeresis/exonym-aware** (München/Munich, Köln/Cologne
  via the curated table + DIN-5007-2 folding) and **conservative** (keeps
  remote / empty / unknown; a country-code-only request is inert). The raw
  board-local matcher had none of that and, running *before* the central filter,
  produced exactly the false drops the central filter exists to prevent (e.g. a
  `"Köln, Deutschland"` request against a `"Cologne"` feed).
- The original 0-results bug is still fixed: `location_spec()` feeds the picker's
  `"<city>, <country>"` label into the central filter, which **tokenises** it (it
  never required the whole label as one substring), so a geocode-picked city
  keeps its rows via the central filter alone.

## Changes

- `common.rs`: `matches_filters` is now **keyword-only** (drops the `location`
  param); `location_matches` is deleted.
- `germantechjobs`: `matches_gtj_filters` is keyword-only; the now-unused `loc`
  local is removed.
- The Muse / Comeet call sites pass query only.
- Tests across `common` / `themuse` / `germantechjobs`: the board-local location
  assertions are replaced with ones pinning that these boards **no longer drop on
  location** (delegated to the central filter, whose own exonym/diacritic
  behaviour is unit-tested in `engine::location_filter`).

Net: **−80 lines**, one authority instead of two.

## Known limitation (unchanged, now consistent across all non-location boards)

Because `location_spec()` stores the whole label in `city`, the central filter
also tokenises the country segment, so `"Munich, Germany"` keeps a
`"Frankfurt, Germany"` row. That is a property of the central filter shared by
**every** non-location board — not something this PR introduces (the old strict
board-local filter merely masked it for these three). Tightening it is a separate
change to `location_filter` / `location_spec`, out of scope here.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p ajh-tauri --lib scraping::` — **883 passed, 0 failed**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `cargo clippy --lib` clean.

<sub>The full monorepo pre-push gate isn't runnable in my sandbox (the landing
build needs an install; Node 26 breaks jsdom `localStorage` in unrelated
frontend suites), so I verified the Rust crate directly as above. CI is
authoritative.</sub>

title: fix: picking a city from the location autocomplete returns 0 results on The Muse and GermanTechJobs
body:
## Describe the bug

Picking a city from the location autocomplete makes **The Muse** and **GermanTechJobs**
return **zero** results. Typing the same city as free text (without picking a suggestion)
works fine — which is why this survives manual testing.

### Root cause

The geocode picker hands the UI a `"<city>, <country>"` label. `commands/geocoding.rs`
builds it deliberately, "so the UI only ever shows 'City, Country'":

```rust
let display = match (&city, &country) {
    (Some(city), Some(country)) => format!("{city}, {country}"),
```

and both entry points write that label straight into the `location` field:

- `ScrapeForm/ScrapeFilters.tsx` — `onSelectSuggestion={(s) => onFormChange({ location: s.display, … })}`
- autopilot `wizard-steps/StepTarget/index.tsx` — `field.onChange(s.display)`

But the two boards that filter location client-side require that **whole label** to be one
contiguous substring of the board's own location text:

`scraping/boards/common.rs` (The Muse + Comeet):
```rust
let loc = posting.location.as_deref().unwrap_or("").to_lowercase();
if !loc.contains(&loc_filter) {
    return false;
}
```

`scraping/boards/germantechjobs/mod.rs`:
```rust
if !loc.is_empty() && !haystack.contains(&loc) {
    return false;
}
```

No board writes its location in the picker's shape, so the match never succeeds:

| board | its own location text | requested label | `contains`? |
|---|---|---|---|
| The Muse | `New York, NY` | `New York, United States` | ✗ |
| GermanTechJobs | `Karl-Marx-Allee 1, Berlin` | `Berlin, Germany` | ✗ |
| GermanTechJobs | `Munich, Bayern` | `Munich, Germany` | ✗ |

Both location shapes above are this repo's own test fixtures
(`themuse/test.rs`, `germantechjobs/test.rs`).

For GermanTechJobs the failure is total and locale-independent: the feed puts the city
**last** in an address or pairs it with a region, never with a country — so every
geocode-picked German city returns 0, which is the entire market that board serves.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Jobs page → click the **Location** box → type `Berlin` → **click the suggestion**
   (`Berlin, Germany`).
2. Select the **German Tech Jobs** (or **The Muse**) board.
3. Scrape.

## Expected behaviour

Berlin postings are returned.

## Actual behaviour

0 results. Re-running with `Berlin` typed but *not* picked returns them normally.

## Note

The engine's own central filter already does the right thing —
`scraping/engine/location_filter.rs` tokenizes the request and keeps a posting on **any**
token hit. Both boards report `supports_location() == false`, so that lenient central filter
is meant to be the authority for them; the strict board-local filter pre-empts it and
returns zero before the engine ever sees a row.

## Environment

- **OS**: macOS (platform-independent)
- **App version**: `main` @ `e1a2295`
